title: Honeybadger Metric
description: A metric recorded through Honeybadger's instrumentation API and flushed by the metrics registry. The metric_type field tells you whether the event came from gauge, increment_counter, decrement_counter, histogram, or time.

metric_name.description: Name of the metric as passed to the recording call.

metric_type.description: The metric type. Timers are gauges recorded via Honeybadger.time.

samples.description: Number of observations recorded in this flush window.

interval.description: Length of the aggregation/flush window in seconds (insights.registry_flush_interval, default 60).

total.description: Sum of all recorded values in the window (gauge, timer, and histogram metrics).

min.description: Minimum recorded value in the window (gauge, timer, and histogram metrics).

max.description: Maximum recorded value in the window (gauge, timer, and histogram metrics).

avg.description: Average of recorded values in the window (gauge, timer, and histogram metrics).

latest.description: Most recently recorded value in the window (gauge, timer, and histogram metrics).

counter.description: Accumulated counter value for the window (counter metrics).

bins.description: Histogram bin counts as [upper_bound, count] pairs. The final bin's upper bound is 1e20, which represents infinity.

request_id.description: Rails request UUID, present on any event fired during a web request context. Set by the Honeybadger agent when available.

hostname.description: Server hostname. Attached to every event by default (events.attach_hostname).

environment.description: Application environment, e.g. "production". Attached to every event by default (events.attach_environment).

metric_source.description: Source plugin or component that recorded the metric, e.g. "rails", "sidekiq", "solid_queue", "net_http", "puma", "autotuner".

additionalProperties.description: Additional dynamic attributes attached to the metric at recording time.
